# Migrating from v1.1.0 to v1.1.1

**Status: Advisory only.** v1.1.0 had no production users. This document is for archivists, contributors, and audit traceability.

## What Changed

v1.1.1 swaps the desktop platform from Flutter to Tauri 2 with React + Vite. Mobile remains on Flutter.

| Component | v1.1.0 | v1.1.1 |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| **Desktop** | Flutter + Dart | Tauri 2 + React + Vite |
| **Mobile** | Flutter (iOS, Android) | Flutter (iOS, Android) — unchanged |
| **Shared protocol** | libnclaw Rust FFI | libnclaw Rust FFI — unchanged |

## Why

Tauri 2 delivers native performance, smaller binary size, and a mature ecosystem for desktop chat UI. See architecture decision ADR-0001 for full reasoning.

## Data Migration

**Zero.** v1.1.0 wrote no persistent data outside the standard local database schema that v1.1.1 reads natively. Uninstall and reinstall with a fresh environment.

## If You Had v1.1.0 Installed

1. Uninstall via your OS's standard uninstaller.
2. Reinstall v1.1.1 fresh.
3. No config files or local data require migration.

## Mobile Users

Unaffected. Flutter mobile codebase continues in `nclaw/mobile/`.

## Legacy Code

Archived in `nclaw/legacy-flutter-desktop/` (read-only historical reference). Removed in v1.2.0.

## Questions?

See the [Getting Started](../Getting-Started.md) guide or open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/nself-org/nclaw).
